Guid
6c59ed92-0840-4a40-8157-c52fc59540f3
8f8bca05-2d6d-4436-99d0-c0cbd1f73b7f
367d3e31-790c-4178-976f-a7e8735d209e
Loading screen
Engwithan waystation
is a
location
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Background
This large facility located on a desert island called
Poko Kohara
in modern tongues was once the factory where Engwithans took
Huana
they lived with and sacrificed them, using their essence to create
luminous adra
for use in their machinery. Long sealed and defended by
Engwithan Titans
, the facility has weathered the passage of time well - enough for a Huana priest to harness the power of the adra pillar for his own ends.
Points of interest
Level one
As usual, you enter the waystation from the southwest. The antechamber is in good shape and has some VTC supplies (
Flint and Tinder
,
Torch
, some miscellaneous consumables). In the corridor beyond is a mural depicting the
Huana
and the
Engwithans
together. The northern corridor leads to a heavily trapped room where the traps are quite lethal. The 11
Caltrops Traps
can each be disarmed with Mechanics 6. The mural in this room depicts both cultures gathering around a blue flame. A hidden brick under it reveals a secret room that contains a pair of unique
Engwithan Bracers
, and a long-forgotten Huana crocodilian idol.
The other side contains a number of enemies, including
Scarab beetles
and
Luminous revenants
, among remnants of a chamber that collapsed under the weight of sand and stone from the outside. The central room contains the tip of a luminous adra pillar, which you can activate to see a small cutscene. This room leads deeper into the station, through a passageway lit with magical fire. More scarabs, and a
Skeletal sorcerer
wait at the bend, together with a small trapped stash near the pillar. The chamber beyond contains the tip of another luminous adra pillar, this time much larger, together with another mural depicting the Huana prostrating themselves before a magical pillar. Upon entering the room, one of your companions will say something witty. Head down the stairs to proceed to the next level.
Level two
You start in the northeast, near the base of the adra pillar that appears to be corrupted somehow. Head to the chamber nearby to find another body belonging to the VTC expedition, together with the
Poko Kohara expedition log
that sheds light on what happened to the expedition. There's also a mural on the wall, depicting an Engwithan sacrifice of a Huana native. With Mechanics 10, you can lockpick the door to the northwest to enter the tomb and skip to level 3, otherwise you'll need to take the long way around.
The pool room to the southeast is filled with
Desert imps
, that come to life as you approach, as well as a single
Greater black ooze
. The long corridor beyond this contains even more imps. The room with the burning torches across the hall breaks up the monotony by offering
Desert spiders
and
Desert spiderlings
. Destroy the stacked gunpowder barrels to reveal a secret tomb with
Marux Amanth
, a soulbound dagger as well as a number of other goodies. The room also contains a body with an
Old Copper Key
.
The vast chamber to the southwest is beautiful, intimidating, and full of skeletons that climb up from the depths. Once you dispose of them and take in the sights, inspect the room to the southeast (mind the traps by the stairs - they can be removed with Mechanics 7). As you clear the threshold, you will be attacked by an
Engwithan saint
and three
Corrupted adra animats
, who will come to life once you enter combat. The room seems to have been used to sacrifice Huana natives to power up the adra within the facility. The body of
Falero
, a member of the VTC expedition lies in the center of the room.
There's another tomb at the end of the grand hall to the west, belonging to
Xakoras
, another Engwithan Saint. He will awaken when you enter the room and attack, spawning revenants, skeletons, and animats. The tomb you could cut through in the beginning is opposite the corridor leading to level three, and contains more animats, as well as an
Adra ooze
. The trapped chest contains minor loot.
Finally, the door to level 3 can be accessed it's defended by a pair of corrupted animats and a sigil of nightmares. If you looted the
Sigil of Nightmares Wardstone
from Falero, now would be a good time to activate it (or simply destroy the sigil). The door is opened with the old copper key, looted from a body in the spider room.
Level three
The deepest level of the waystation. In the center is an electric trap that sends out bolts of electricity (giving you
System Shock
, -2 Perception, -3 Shock Armor Rating) unless you take out the essence batteries surrounding it. More importantly, the body of
Beza
is in the southwest, smashed to death by some unspoken horror. You can read her soul to get an idea of what happened, as well as collect
Beza's pages
, a
Ngati Idol
, and her weapon,
Beza's Toothed Blade
. Beyond that, a mural on the wall depicts the Huana sacrifices appearing to supply the pillar with energy. Make your way down the southeastern corridor to the pillar of corrupted adra to finalize
The Storms of Poko Kohara
.
A staircase on the northeastern side of the room exits directly to
Poko Kohara ruins
, opening a shortcut to level three. Otherwise, interacting with the corrupted adra pillar opens a rift to
The Adra Realm
.

Esternwood
Location ID
AR_0706_Esternwood
Loading screen
Esternwood
is a wilderness area in
Pillars of Eternity
. It's located east of
Gilded Vale
.
Points of interest
There is graveyard at the north part of the area, which contains some of the
backer memorials
found throughout the game.
During
The Grey Sleeper
, the Watcher can rest in the graveyard to solve the second riddle and progress the quest.
Otherwise, this is a straight route to
Raedric's Hold
.
Characters
Kolsc
, shows up as you approach the eastern border to give you the quest
Lord of a Barren Land
. He disappears in a cutscene immediately after giving the quest. Note that he won't show up if he already gave the quest in
Gilded Vale
.
Thorfen
, during the quest
Bounty: Thorfen
.
Companion reactions
Some companions have a special comment upon visiting the graveyard for the first time:
Aloth
: '"Many of these are quite recent. Someone's been busy."
Kana Rua
:
"A great many of these people died just this past year... Not of plague, I think."
Durance
:
"I say the Eothasian leads the way."
Grieving Mother
:
"The work of a community... ignorant and vengeful."
Edér
:
"They probably got one dug for me too. Hope they gave me a nice spot."

Behind the scenes
This area contains developer commentary, however there is no way to trigger it in-game.
“
This road was kept purposefully peaceful, we wanted a buffer between Gilded Vale and the stuff going on in Raedric's Hold. At one point Kolsc was meant to approach you in town, after you'd done a couple of side-quests, so there were a couple of snarky guards out here patrolling along the road and they would give the player more information about the Hold. We didn't really want to gate Raedric's Hold off however, so we ended up moving Kolsc out here and gave him all the exposition that the guards used to have.
”
~
Olivia Veras, Junior Designer
https://pillarsofeternity.fandom.com/wiki/File:Olivia_veras_esternwood.ogg
Developer commentary by Olivia Veras
Map
Map - Esternwood

Felipe's Depression
is a landmark in
Maxim's Claim
, a subregion of
Emerald Stair
in
Avowed
.
Background
A massive sinkhole under the now-destroyed workshop and residence of
Old Felipe
, leading to an ancient
Godless
ruin buried in the earth. The recent growth of giant roots had destabilised the soil under the property, leading to the sinkhole's sudden and dramatic formation.
Points of interest
Characters
Old Felipe
, at the bottom of the sinkhole.

Fior mes Ivèrno
(
Vailian
:
Flower of Winter
), more commonly known as
Fior
(sometimes
Fiore
), is a major
Vailian
settlement of animancers in the
Emerald Stair
region of the
Living Lands
in
Avowed
.
Background
See:
Maxim Vidarro
Fior mes Ivèrno was founded around 2780
AI
by
Maxim Vidarro
, a former Vailian nobleman who led a group of animancers from Spirento to the Living Lands. Under his leadership the settlement quickly flourished as an animancy commune, developing independently from the
Vailian Republics
. Leadership eventually passed to his son,
Ignasi Vidarro
, continuing the legacy of Fior mes Ivèrno amidst the encroaching threat of the
Dreamscourge
.
Points of interest
Animancy Workshop
Bone & Boar
Castell Family Lab
Essence of War
Giatta's House
The Gifted Magpie
Quilicci's House
Piazia mes Vidarro
Porrya & Vellino
Vidarro Family Mansion
Characters
Merchants
Abritt Porrya
(Porrya & Vellino), sells monster parts, grimoires, the unique grimoire
Minoletta's Conduit
.
Ector Brewer
(The Gifted Magpie), sells various food and drink,
Treasure Map - Practical Pockets (item)
, and
Warmage's Bracers
.
Merchant Stephan
(Essence of War), sells weapons, the unique mace
Lodestone
.
Xavi Brewer
(Bone and Boar Bar), sells alcohol and bar foods.
Trivia
Fior is home to
Giatta
and
Yatzli
, two companions in the game.
Behind the scenes
Fior mes Ivèrno was first mentioned in the
Giatta Cosplay Guide
as
Giatta
's birthplace, published on January 18, 2023.
The fire of Fior was first mentioned during the Developer_Direct Extended on January 22, 2023, in dialogue with
Hasui
, he alludes to something notable happening in Fior.
Later in the Story Trailer during the Xbox Games Showcase on June 6, 2024, Fior is shown on fire.
Carrie Patel, game director on
Avowed
, first describes Fior during the Xbox Games Showcase Deep Dive, on June 10, 2024.
Gallery
Map of Fior
Fior, set ablaze
A Vailian banner burning (a ship on water below the five stars representing the 5 great cities of the Republics)
References

🗺️
First Fires
Location ID
AR_0201_First_Fires_Exterior
Loading screen
“
Politics, infighting, greed. And a shrine to Magran as well. Her clergy belongs there.
”
~
Durance
First Fires
is the government district of
Defiance Bay
in
Pillars of Eternity
.
Background
The government district, named as such to commemorate it as the place where the revolution started. It hosts the Ducal Palace, Vailian embassy, and Crucible Keep. An old, derelict temple of Woedica is also present, deliberately left to rot and decay.
Points of interest
This is a central hub map, connecting all four of the main districts of
Defiance Bay
. In the center is a
crier
, the local news network, standing next to a ruined tower, otherwise the notable characters are located inside the buildings.
To the north is the
Ducal Palace
, seat of power for the rulers of the
Free Palatinate of Dyrwood
and the current
Duc Aevar Wolf-Grin
.
To the east is
Crucible Keep
, the
Knights of the Crucible
's main base of operations within the
Defiance Bay
and surrounding areas.
To the west is the
Vailian Embassy
, residence and offices of
Vicent Agosti
, the
Vailian Republics
' ambassador to
The Dyrwood
.
Approach the
Temple of Woedica
and you can encounter a ghost belonging to lord
Adwellen Rügfald III
, who is a crucial part of the main quest.
Characters
Adwellen Rügfald III
, the ghost of.
Backer NPCs
Kad'lr Koan
Llyranor
Meghnad
Quinalin Grapplewind
Ravadav Durr
Ruben Torres
"Twelvecharms" Maggie Tiller
Urist Nakazul
Companion reactions
Some companions have a special comment upon visiting the
Temple of Woedica
:
Aloth
:
"This looks like the place
Maerwald
described."
Sagani
:
"Is this the old church you were looking for?"
Kana Rua
:
"This would be the temple Maerwald spoke of. Or what remains of it."
Hiravias
:
"This was once hallowed ground for
Woedica
's faithful. I wonder how long it's been abandoned..."
Edér
:
"This'd be our church of Woedica. Heh,
Dyrwoodans
. Probably the first thing we burned during the revolution."
Two companions have a special comment upon reaching the
Ducal Palace
:
Durance
:
"Politics, infighting, greed. And a shrine to
Magran
as well. Her clergy belongs there."
Grieving Mother
:
"Too many... minds here, all shallow."
Durance has a special comment upon reaching the
Vailian Embassy
:
Durance
:
"All these circling feet, heading nowhere."

Behind the scenes
The First Fires district was likely going to have four additional "house" locations, as evidenced by the unused area IDs:
AR_0202_First_Fires_House_01
,
AR_0203_First_Fires_House_02
,
AR_0204_First_Fires_House_03
,
AR_0205_First_Fires_House_04_Lower
, and
AR_0206_First_Fires_House_04_Upper
. It's possible that these were moved to
Copperlane
at some point during development.

Fishery
Location ID
PX1_0004_Fishery
The
Fishery
is a building in
Stalwart Village
.
Background
The backbone of
Stalwart
's economy, the fishery is where the bounty gathered in the lakes of the
White March
is processed and prepared for shipping and distribution.
Points of interest
The building consists of a single, long room with two containers by the entrance and a single fishery worker sweeping the floor in the center. The container in the back holds
Arda's Bracelet
.
Characters
Note:
Only during the daytime
Clerk
, selling the day's latest catches.
Grynde
, head fisherman.
During
The Fisherman's Penance
Lurgolder
, fisherman (only if you saved him from the burning house).

[WM1]

.

Flames-That-Whisper Caverns
is a network of caverns in
The White March - Part I
, and the seat of the eponymous crag ogre clan.
Background
These winding caves are used as shelter for the Flames-That-Whisper crag ogre clan. They are filled with their bedrolls, trophies, and the odd carved skull.
Points of interest
Upper cavern
An area filled with ogres. The entrance to the lower level is to the far northeast. The corridor on the northeastern corner of the map contains a large camp with several ogres and canoneers, so be wary. To the southeast is yet another camp with more ogres, pondering cuisine. All containers are marked as owned.
In the center of the map is a large opening that you can interact with. This opens up a scripted interaction where you can use a
grappling hook
to climb down
Half-way down you will find an outcropping with a traveler's bones. At this point you can try to swing for the outcropping, but doing so will cause the hook to give way. A character with
Dexterity
16 and isn't an Aumaua can climb up the chute to re-secure the hook. This will allow you to take
Golden Gaze
from a bag buried among the bones.
If you ignore the outcropping, you can just keep on climbing down to arrive at the lower level.
If you end up in free-fall, a priest with
Armor of Faith
can protect the party from damage (bruised ribs for all). Having
Dragon Leap
unlocked allows you to protect yourself.
Behind the locked door is the scrying room. You need the key from the fishery to access it and confront the matron.
Lower cavern
The lower level is populated by
lagufaeths
and is divided into several discrete sections. If you use the stairs, you start in the eastern part with the ogre armories a short walk to the north and the south. The rest of the area is connected by a single central chamber with magical pillars.
The ogre mine is in the northern part of this level.
The mine contains a
Severed Horn
that allows you to connect to the soul of
Garodh
and start
Garodh's Chorus
(consult the page for the meaning of the choices).
There is an interactive ore vein on the eastern edge. Using a
Hammer and Chisel
and passing an
attribute
check (
player character
needs
Dexterity
15+) will yield 7×
Velune
and 2×
Topaz
.
The north-western corridor is trapped with a pair of magical pillars linked to a tripwire. Mechanics 11 (12 on POTD) will see you safely across them. The two-trap corridor to the northwest leads to a room with ice blights and stairs leading to the matron's chambers.
The south-western section has a large central pool that acts as the ogre fishery. The amphibians patrol its edges, with the exit point for the ice chute to the south-east. The hidden gap near the glowing pillars has the unique
Wayfarer's Hide
. Check the bodies of the ogres for the
scrying chamber key
.
Notes
It's possible to pass through this area in relative peace without killing too many ogres. To do so, sneak to the ice chute, make your way down, find the scrying room key, then return the same way and confront the matron directly.
If you forge a truce,
Morug
will become available as a trader at the entrance to the cave.
Characters
Matron Beregan
: The leader of the ogre tribe.

======================================================================
PAGE: Fleetbreaker Castle
CATEGORIES: Pillars of Eternity locations
URL: https://pillarsofeternity.fandom.com/wiki/Fleetbreaker_Castle
======================================================================

Fleetbreaker Castle
Details
Game
Pillars of Eternity
Locations
Part of
The Dyrwood
Fleetbreaker Castle
is the headquarters of the
Knights of the Crucible
, also known as Daghan's Spire.
History
It was once known for its corps of crossbow defenders - the Boghadir. These elite guards were renowned for their deadly accuracy and carried powerful steel crossbows such as this. The Boghadir battle doctrine was twofold. First, any invading force would come under fire by designated marksmen at range. These marksmen, firing at will, were to diminish and demoralize the attackers. Second - when their enemies closed distance to the walls of the keep, Boghadir point-defenders would then hit them with relentless, coordinated mass-fire, one line after another. If the attackers were repulsed and fell back, they would once again fall under fire from the sharpshooters. At no point was the enemy to have any respite from a steady rain of crossbow bolts.
By 2823 AI, it's the headquarters of the Crucible Knights and the seat of power of the High Justice, the leader of the faction.

Flooded Cave
Details
Game
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire

Allows rest?
Technical

AR_2605_Lich_Lair
Guid
1a5a8736-8823-4c31-8f1c-3156eb5365fb
Flooded Cave
is a
location
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
. It is located on an island in the
Ofecchia Channel
, in the far northeastern corner of the map, and at the coordinates
4° 27′ S, 65° 17′ E
.
Background
As you traverse the island, you arrive at an area that appears to be untouched by kith hands.
Lush plants grow in riotous abundance around the entrances to several caves, and you can hear the sounds of animals and insects over the crash of waves on the beach behind you.When you walk closer, however, you notice that one of the cave entrances is too uniformly structured to have been created by nature alone.
Inspecting it reveals a doorway carved into the aperture of the cave, though a door no longer stands there now.
Points of interest
This is a large cave filled with vessels controlled by an ancient battlemage lich. Wiping them out is perfectly doable, with the best results obtained by luring them away from the lich's spires and fighting them near the entrance.
There is a sigil of pain in the cabinet nearest to the spire of pain, making it convenient to sneak to the cabinet first, then use the sigil to begin combat. Or, you can target the pillar with a long range attack, taking it out first before dealing with the denizens.
Loot
Brawler's Grimoire
: Dropped by the ancient lich battlemage. One of three grimoires required for the quest
The Lost Grimoires
.
Blackwood Log
in a cabinet.
Superb Quarterstaff, Superb Sabre, Superb Great Sword, Superb Morning Star, and several pieces of superb armor.

Forgotten Catacomb
Details

40b7a263-9466-40b3-b151-68bda457cfc7
Forgotten Catacomb
is a
location
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
. It is located on an unmarked island at the coordinates
21° 16′ S, 51° 12′ E
.
Tangaloa's Maw
is to the east.
Background
As the name suggests, this is a forgotten underground burial site in the middle of nowhere.
A lush, narrow valley lies below you. You spot an overgrown stone structure build into the valley's side in the distance.
Before the stone structure spreads a broad field peppered with ancient plinths and mounds of cracked stonework.
As you move closer, you discover that the stone structure is actually the entrance to a crypt. Though the doorway is thick with mud and as damp as sweat-slick skin, you continue down into the dark.
Points of interest
This isn't a big location: The central throne room is where the
Death Guard
fanatic has established his lair, gathering various valuables and other baubles. He drops the
Reckless Brigandine
. Oh, and there's also undead all over.
In the center is a Sigil of Atrophy, which applies
Curse of Atrophy
(-2 Might, stacks up to 5 times). Destroy it from a distance, or use a
Sigil of Atrophy Wardstone
to mitigate it's effects. There are traps surrounding it which causes it to attack if stepped on, these attacks cause
Serious Burn
(-2 Dexterity, -3 Burn Armor Rating). When the sigil is destroyed, the traps are deactivated.
Upon leaving the catacombs, you get to name the island as part of
Mapping the Archipelago: Tikawara
.
Enemies
Wilder
Revenant ogre
(x2)
Vessel
Fampyr
(x2)
Dargul
(x5)
Death Guard Fanatic
Other
Sigil of Atrophy
: In the center.
Loot
Looted from the Death Guard Fanatic
Reckless Brigandine
Exceptional War Bow
Exceptional Battle Axe
Exceptional Mace
Other
Scroll of Kalakoth's Minor Blights
,
Spire Sponge
: Central area, in a hidden Urn.
Potion of Improved Arcane Reflection
: Central area, sarcophagus.
Gallery
The entrance to the catacombs.
The island containing Forgotten Catacomb

Fort Deadlight
is a
location
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Background
A former Vailian fortification claimed a few years back by Captain Aeldys of the Príncipi, after clearing out the horde of wilders that took it over and drove the Vailians away. She uses it as her staging area now, and a hideout for those Príncipi captains who fall in behind her. The Fort is notable for the massive guns that crown its numerous turrets, ensuring that any who would try their luck dislodging the Príncipi will be going back home shot up to hell and back.
Points of interest
Before completing
Blow the Man Down
,
Fort Deadlight
is strictly guarded. See
the quest
for the details of the security.
You enter at the gate to the east.
The stairs near the gate - guarded by
Unlucky Ancret
- lead to the upper ramparts.
Up north is the door to
Deadlight donjon
, locked.
Due west is the stairs to the
secret dock
.
No-Nut Ned
set up his shop at the southeastern corner.
At the center of the map, the stairs up lead to
Deadlight Court
, while the stairs down lead to
Deadlight dungeon
.
Sceydwin
and
Minao
are tied to the posts nearby.
Going further west, you see the drunken pirate -
Ruddy Feyr
. The stairs nearby - guarded by
Ungwith the Craghearted
- lead to the door to the forge.
Characters
Snake Eyes Condwen
, a dice game-runner near the entrance of the fort.
Unlucky Ancret
, a rather poor dice player who guards the Eastern Ramparts.
Sceydwin
, a pirate punished by Benweth.
Minao
, a captive lashed to a post.
Ruddy Feyr
, a drunken pirate.
Merchants
No-Nut Ned
, a vendor of several commodities, fine weapons, and unique armors.
Loot
Hati
- "Neglected Dog": On the left immediately after entering the area.
Ludde
- "Forsaken Cat": North past of the eastern ramparts.
The Bloody Links
: Sold by Ned.

Details

Foundations of the Ashen Maw
is a
location
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Background
The foundations of the Maw go deep below the Keep, directly into the volcanic rock and the lifeblood of the Earth.
Eothas
is here now, siphoning the energy of the luminous adra pillar at the heart of Magran's Teeth.
Points of interest
This is a vista, giving you a close look at Eothas. To progress, simply approach him and start buzzing near his ears.

Fractured Shore
is a subregion of
Dawnshore
in
Avowed
.
Background
The smallest of the subregions of
Dawnshore
, Fractured Shore is a coastal area on the western edge of the region, characterized by tall, unstable cliffs rising from the ocean. These cliffs are fragmented and separated, with a series of aging, makeshift bridges connecting them.
Points of interest
Dehengen's Cottage
, a house on the cliffs, recently invaded by a band of xaurips.

“

~
Desthwn
, leader of the
Fangs
Galawain's Maw
is a temple to
Galawain
, the lord of the hunt. It is located in the
Oldsong
district of
Twin Elms
.
Background
The headquarters of the
Fangs
, the finest hunters of
Eir Glanfath
, represents a mix between Engwithan and Glanfathan architecture. It doubles as a temple dedicated to
Galawain
(thus the name). The Fangs gathered here currently eagerly await the the fight between
Oernos
and
Sul
,
The Old Queen and the New King
. At other times, the Maw is the heart of the Fangs' operations, where they train, live, eat, and prepare to defend Eir Glanfath from foreigners.
Points of interest
This is a compact area. You enter it via the foyer, filled with Fangs and their companions.
Einden
will have a short conversation with another Fang as you approach, then move to wait under the column.
Larha
stands in the small, sand-filled room to the south.
The opposite chamber is called the Fang's heart and has
Desthwn
, the leader of the faction, nervously pacing across the tiled floor. Talk to him to receive the key opening all locked doors in the Maw.
The chambers of the beasts are vast and generally filled with lion clansmen and lions.
Sul
is found in the northeastern part of the area together with her ally.
Oernos
appears once you talk to the lioness and decide what to do.
Characters
Einden
and
Oernos
Larha
, the merchant
Desthwn
Irensî
and
Sul

for completing

, or can be looted from him.

Behind the scenes
A nasty bug may appear when you enter the area, you may become stuck in combat mode (against various units that may not even present anymore), which can't be cured by using an earlier savegame. In this case, you have to turn on "autosave when entering new areas" and when you are in combat, reload this savegame. Combat will be disabled on load, and you can continue.
This area contains developer commentary.
“
Headquarter of the Fangs, and sacred natural shrine to Galawain, the Maw blends Engwithan architecture and organic shapes, while also being a pretty compact area. It was a design challenge that nevertheless I believe turned out quite well. In general the more you mix different of space and gameplay, the more difficult it will be to keep everything feeling of a piece [?]. "Keep it simple" is definitely a good advice that applies well to game design.
This beautiful render was the work of Holly Prado, and it really brought the mystical and natural side of this area into focus.
”
~
Jorge Salgado, Designer
https://pillarsofeternity.fandom.com/wiki/File:Jorge_salgado_galawains_maw.ogg
Developer commentary by Jorge Salgado

Galawain's Tusks
is mountainous volcanic region of the
Living Lands
, in
Avowed
.
Solace
is the major settlement in the area, an old Pargrun keep. It is the fourth region visited by
The Envoy
, after
Shatterscarp
.
Background
The Tusks' is known as one of the most harsh and uninabitable areas in the Lands'. Central to the region is a large active volcano, which can be seen billowing steam and spewing volcanic ash from miles around.
The area is home to a group of isolationist
Pargrunen
dwarves, whose stronghold protects them from the deadly creatures roaming the area.
Points of interest
Locations
Solace Keep
, an isolated
Pargrun
keep, a settlement of
aptapo
mountain dwarves
.

🗺️
Galvino's Workshop
Location ID
PX1_0206_Galvino_Workshop_02
Loading screen
Galvino's Workshop
is a dungeon in
The White March - Part I
expansion of
Pillars of Eternity
.
Background
A large subterranean facility hidden beneath an unassuming log cabin in the woods. Galvino, the eccentric engineer and animancer has adapted it for his various experiments and studies.
Points of interest
You start in the southwest. The first corridor is filled with traps, with the next room containing flesh and misshapen constructs.
Galvino's old chambers are to the east of the first chamber you enter. After a corridor with two traps you run into a horde of misshapen, aggressive constructs. It connects to two more rooms, one is Galvino's old study and dining room at the far end (pilfer
experimental notes
and
workshop key
from the shelves). The old workshop has more constructs (surprise) and more
notes
.
The room to the west contains more damaged constructs,
Galvino
's failed forays into dwarven automaton technology. Be wary of unstable ones, they will attempt to self-detonate near your party and killing them before the skill goes off is the only way to avoid a large explosion. The room is filled with remains of experiments, with the
Subject Roster
on the table near the entrance.
The wall has a hidden switch (revealed with
Mechanics
9 in stealth) that opens into Galvino's old private chambers and random loot on the armor rack. The locked cell contains a broken construct with a still-awake soul inside that will try to extort you... If you choose an option which leads to the construct realizing what it is, it will attack you; if you simply pay the fee (
500
), it will be incapable of receiving the coins (you still lose it anyways) but will leave you alone afterwards; with an
Intellect
of 13, you can make it aware of its cage and the open door, so it will leave and self-destruct.
The area beyond the workroom contains a large room with several machines that Galvino used in his experiments with souls and their transfers. Oh, and it's filled with more constructs, including a pair of exploding ones that will rush you the moment you step inside.
Baelsyr
is locked inside the barrel to the left, with
Subject: Baelsyr
on the shelves behind.
The eastern door can be activated using a lever. The winding corridor leads to Galvino's old proving grounds. The part to the northeast contains the old construct proving grounds with more exploding constructs and one carrying
crumpled experimental notes
. The table in the back contains the
cell key
.
The final chamber is accessed with the
workshop key
. Inside is
Galvino
and the
Devil of Caroc
.
Characters
Galvino
, a Vailian animancer.
Devil of Caroc
, recruitable companion construct.
Baelsyr
, spirit of a robber.
Alryn Razorfingers
, a broken construct who attempts to extort you.
Enemies
Vessels:
Constructs
Burning Flesh Construct
Flesh Construct
Iron Construct
Misshapen Construct
Rotting Flesh Construct
Unstable Construct
Loot
Notable loot
Fellstroke
: A unique
pistol
, in the laboratory.
Echoing Misery
: Hidden in the cell with the broken construct.

🗺️
Gilded Vale
Location ID
AR_0705_Gilded_Vale
Loading screen
Gilded Vale
is a village in
Pillars of Eternity
, situated in
The Grasp
erldom of the
Dyrwood
.
Background
Gilded Vale is the heart of the northwestern grain basket in the
Free Palatinate of Dyrwood
. It lies in the erldom known as the Grasp (formerly Yenwood) just past the eastern edge of the Helsgate erldom. Though it used to see very high traffic during the early colonial days, Gilded Vale has settled into a quieter pace of life as colonization and trade have pushed farther east. Notably, it was beset by a particularly territorial fire dragon in the early years of the Dyrwood's colonial period. It was believed that the beast was provoked by the construction of Raedric's Hold nearby. The first Lord Raedric cemented his popularity – and that of his house – when he gathered a dozen of his finest soldiers and slew the dragon himself. The slaying was commemorated with a special armor crafted from its scales, which was worn by his descendants in the War of Defiance, against the Empire.
More recently, this small hamlet north of the Pearlwood Gulf  was once at the center of a sizable Eothasian community in Dyrwood. However, the events of the
Saint's War
resulted in the Vale's decline and depopulation, as they were forced out of the area and the holy temple destroyed. To prevent the total depopulation of the Vale, Lord
Raedric VII
has brought in settlers with promises of wealth and land. Although many graciously accepted the invitation and took over the Vale's abandoned homesteads, they usually give the Vale a wide berth to avoid even the remote risk of being associated with a former Eothasian community.
The Hollowborn Crisis affected the already straining  Vale severely and Lord Raedric's deteriorating mental state led to a series of purges among the villagers. The once grand tree of the Temple of Eothas became the local gallows, adorned with rotting corpses.
Points of interest
This is a fairly large, well-populated map, so let's get straight to exploring. Head right down the  road to reach the tree that once grew in the temple of Eothas, now serving as the local gallows. You'll be immediately accosted by
Urgeat
, who will question you on any
Hollowborn
ties and "welcome" you to the town. After the conversation ends, three bells will toll, signifying the death of a
Raedric
. The Lord's son was hollowborn and everything gets a bit complicated.
After everything's said and done, head around the tree to the sound and find
Edér
. He will not be
recruitable
immediately. First you need to rest at the
Black Hound Inn
and talk to the
dead animancer dwarf
on the tree (apparently, talking to dead people piques his interest), as part of the
Visions and Whispers
quest.
The Black Hound
inn
is in the south-western corner. As you approach, three men will harass an elf named
Aloth
, trying to pick a fight with the obviously
Aedyran
national. There are many peaceful options here, namely "
I don't think attacking...
", followed by a
Perception
13 option "
He's reaching for something...
", which prompts the villagers to scatter. Otherwise, they shouldn't be hard to dispatch. Talk to Aloth afterwards for a bit of exposition and the ability to recruit him for your
party
once you exit dialogue. The innkeeper
Pasca
will also ask you to
help find her cook
.
Aufra's House
is located just a little bit north of the inn. Inside,
Aufra
will ask you for
help with her unborn baby
.
The center of the city, apart from the exquisitely decorate tree, has the
temple of Eothas
(or what's left of it), which is a two-level dungeon best tackled with both Edér and Aloth in your party.
The south-eastern corner hosts the
Black Hammer Smithy
, where
Tuatanu
works and another
quest
can be picked up.
The north-eastern corner is occupied by the
windmill
, where
Trumbel
and
Sweynur
are engaged in a vicious fight over grain. Approaching the area begins
Against the Grain
.
Just below the windmill lies
Ingroed's House
.
Characters
Companions

, a
Wood elf
Wizard
.

, a
Meadow folk
Fighter
. Joinable after you've completed
Visions and Whispers
.

and

,

, and

, the local criminal element.

, after you have successfully finished the task
Late for Dinner
.

,
Cearu
and
Ufdaen
, the windmill owner and his two child.

, the local magistrate. In Path of the Damned, Urgeat is also in company of a Guardsman

and

: Just outside

.

Behind the scenes
This area contains developer commentary.
“
It's usually best to leave the beginning of the game for last, so this area was designed in the advanced stages of development. The idea here was to convey a run-down, bleak, farmers' town that suffers from the iron rule of a local warlord - Raedric is the name - whose hall lies nearby and it's also its own adventure area. Here in Gilded Vale it was important to give a glimpse of how bad things really were in this world right away, so I made sure to design the layout in such a way that the tree with the hanging bodies became one of the first things to appear in view when you first arrived to town.
Another important buildings to introduce you included an inn - 'cause you need an inn somewhere at the start of an RPG, that's just expected - places to shop and tinker, like the blacksmith and the mill, and also places that would illuminate background history like the ruins of the Eothasian temple which features in the general narrative that we constructed in the areas quests and characters.
”
~
Jorge Salgado, Designer
https://pillarsofeternity.fandom.com/wiki/File:Jorge_salgado_gilded_vale_exterior.ogg
Developer commentary by Jorge Salgado

Glowing Outcrop
is a
location
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Background
The peak and the vantage point from which
Menzzago
rules over his domain across the
Splintered Reef
, decorated with coffins and even a dining table.
A glowing sphere of energy in the center of the area serves as a focusing point for the spell of illusion that is cast over the reef and its people.
Points of interest
A small area, where you have the sacrificial pool used by Menzzago to keep his vessels active. After dispatching him, you can shatter the orb to turn the undead into mindless husks again.
Characters
Menzzago
, master of this strange domain.

PAGE: Gorecci Street
CATEGORIES: Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire locations
URL: https://pillarsofeternity.fandom.com/wiki/Gorecci_Street
======================================================================

Gorecci Street
Details
Game
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
Type
District
Locations
Part of
Port Maje
People
Inhabitants
Ilari
Quests
Restoring Order
Miscellaneous
Level
2
Explore XP
10
Critical path
Allows rest?
Technical
Zone ID
zn_09_port_maje
Area ID
AR_0916_Port_Maje_Street
Guid
680a2ca7-c3da-418d-b4b0-be2db6bd4b2b
Gorecci Street
is a
location
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Background
As you make your way along through the side streets of Port Maje, you come upon visible traces of the storm's passing.
Many of the houses here boast cracked window panes and sagging awnings, and you pass narrow streets strewn with foliage and debris.
The cobblestones beneath your feet are still slick with water and silt.
Before long you are ankle-deep in sucking mud and loose stones.
The flooding only worsens up ahead, where the water level rises to the knees. Most of Port Maje's residents have fled this region for dry land.
But the murmur of raised voices ahead, and the occasional splash, suggest that you are not entirely alone.
A street in the western part of
Port Maje
, Gorecci has been hit by the flooding in the wake of Eothas' passing.  The flooding damaged most of the buildings in the area and forced residents to flee for dry land, creating a ripe opportunity for unscrupulous scavengers and looters.
Points of interest
This is a small area representing the looted sections of Port Maje. If you arrived from the north, move forward to meet a pair of looters who will immediately attack you unless you successfully sneak past them. They can be disposed of without penalty. If you arrived from the south you can walk past them without them catching up with you even if you aren't sneaking.
Ilari
and his gang are found in the northwestern corner.
Two souls which can be harvested by
Xoti
, otherwise can be asked follow you.
Characters
Ilari
, the local gang leader
Loot
Protective Eothasian Charm
: Hidden in the bucket in the well, requires
Perception
13 to detect.
Gallery
SI graphic

Great Hall
Details
Game
Pillars of Eternity
Type

🗺️
Great Hall
Location ID
AR_0604_Stronghold_Great_Hall
Main Keep
Upgrade to
Caed Nua
Requirement
None

1,400

3 days
Prestige
+3

Upgrade ID
MainKeep
This article is about the location in
Pillars of Eternity
. For the location in
The White March - Part I
and
The White March - Part II
, see
The Great Hall
.
The
Great Hall
is the heart of the
Caed Nua
keep in
Pillars of Eternity
. To repair it the
Main Keep
upgrade
must be purchased.
Description
Upgrading the Main Keep will repair your Great Hall, allowing visitors and adventures to be available to you and your companions.
Background
The heart of all commerce and activity in the keep (once, at any rate), the Great Hall fell into disrepair under
Maerwald
's care.
Points of interest
When you first enter, it's in a state of ruin, like most of Caed Nua. Restoring it with an
upgrade
allows you to receive
adventures
and get
visitors
When upgraded with
Merchant Stalls
, you can purchase
weapons
and
armor
from a
merchant
in a room on the east side.
In the same room as the merchant there is a
chest
on the northeastern wall. This is where the following appear:
Treasure from adventures
Plant
ingredients from the
Botanical Gardens
Purchases from visitors
Just north of the main entrance are the stairs up to the
Barracks
.
The door to the
Library
is located just east of the main entrance.
The stairs down to the
Dungeons
are just north of the
Steward
. The keep was built directly over the
Endless Paths of Od Nua
, which can be accessed through the Dungeons.
Characters
Steward
: On the northeastern wall is a statue of a woman with antlers. In the
main quest
The Old Watcher
you discover that she is the castle
Steward
.
General Goods Merchant
: Next to the treasury after completion of the
Merchant Stalls
Upgrade.
Companion reactions
Some companions have a special comment upon entering the hall:
Edér
:
"Do you think he'd be mad if we sat in his chair? He doesn't have to know."
Aloth
:
"Well, that's... unusual."
Kana Rua
:
"Clearly of Aedyran make... They like their archways."
Hiravias
:
"This place reeks of mildew and rotting wood. They don't build things to last anymore... not like the Engwithans did..."
Pallegina
:
"Such an ominous throne for so humble a keep..."
Sagani
:
"I wouldn't sit there if I were you."
Loot
Note:
For a complete list of
random loot
found in Great Hall, see
here
.
Whispers of Yenwood
: In a vase in the main keep.
Behind the scenes
This area contains developer commentary, describing the various uses of the hall during gameplay.
“
This is the great hall of the stronghold. There's a lot of work going on behind the scenes here. Much like the exterior of the stronghold, all the content of this area had to serve multiple purposes, and be conditionalized by various stages this area can be experienced in.
When the player enters this scene, it is supposed to look like it is in complete shambles and haunted. There is combat in this level and the scene with the Steward of Caed Nua. Once the player unlocks the stronghold and upgrades this area however, it becomes a warm, grand, and inviting area where visitors will appear. On top of all of this, the level also had to serve the purpose of a battle arena, and instances where the player stronghold has been infiltrated by the monsters of the Endless Paths.
All this was accomplished by making the great hall a rather long hall with a great lane in the middle that serves combat tactically. Coincidentally it makes the great all that much more great once it has been fully upgraded.
”
~
Matthew Perez, Junior Designer
https://pillarsofeternity.fandom.com/wiki/File:Matt_perez_stronghold_great_hall.ogg
Developer commentary by Matthew Perez

Behind the scenes
This area contains developer commentary, explaining the characterization of
Korgrak
the ogre.
“
Korgrak's lair was another level during the vertical slice phase of development. This scene was made to evoke the familiar but always fun scenario you might find in a Dungeons & Dragons campaign: there's an ogre bothering a local town, go find his dark, foreboding cave and kill him. However, with Pillars, we always like to subvert the player's expectations where we can. In this instance you have the trappings of your typical ogre lair, fit with piles of bones and the smell of death, but once you actually encounter Korgrak, you quickly learn he's not some dumb creature. He's rather perceptive of your intentions, and has his own motivations for stealing Rumbald's pigs.
Like many of the creatures in our game, we try to put a different spin on the typical fantasy characterization. Ogres in Eora are intelligent creatures, that through their own paranoia of others - especially their own kind - live lives as solitude, and are prone to agitation and outbursts of anger. Korgrak is no different, and we aim to make this conversation with him reflect this. If the player understood this right away, and choose to treat the ogre as a rational creature, they'll be rewarded with avoiding a rather difficult encounter.
”
~
Matthew Perez, Junior Designer
https://pillarsofeternity.fandom.com/wiki/File:Matt_perez_ogre_cave.ogg
Developer commentary by Matthew Perez
Map
Map - Grisly Cave
Gallery

PAGE: Hadret House

Hadret House
Details
Game
Pillars of Eternity
Type
Residence
Locations
Part of
Brackenbury
Exits to
Brackenbury
People
Inhabitants
Lady Webb
Kurren
Quests

Rogue Knight
The Assassin at Large
The Hermit of Hadret House
Technical
Map
🗺️
️Hadret House - Lower
🗺️
️Hadret House - Upper
Location ID
AR_0402_Brackenbury_Hadret_House_Lower
AR_0403_Brackenbury_Hadret_House_Upper
Hadret House
is a building in
Brackenbury
that houses
Lady Webb
's intelligence services protecting
Defiance Bay
.
Background
The former manor of
Duke Admeth
and his father, Hadret House was selected as the headquarters of the
Dunryd Row
, the intelligence service of the Free Palatinate of
Dyrwood
. Among the luxurious furnishings and furniture, the state's greatest ciphers work tirelessly to ensure the security of the colonial nation.
The house itself is a subject of controversy, honeycombed as it is with secret passages and entrances that stretch seemingly across the city. For a municipal building, it offers little in the way of transparency.
Points of interest
Note: Once you are asked to arrive at the Hadret House by Lady Webb's messenger, approaching the building in Brackenbury will result in an attack by six assassins. Furthermore, you will receive another vision of a past life as
Thaos
' missionary, where the old man inquires as to your neglect of your duties. It turns out that the Watcher's past self wanted to leave the order, giving you the ability to define just what happened: Horror at what the fresh converts were doing, sacrificing and mutilating themselves, family abandoning them, doing something unforgivable, or being attacked. Either way, Thaos will use his silver tongue to convince you to remain in the order and be a part of his
Inquisition
.
The lower floor is oriented around a central war room with
Kurren
. The adjoining chambers contain a mess hall and armory to the south-west, another armory to the northwest, and an adjacent break room with vintages older than even Defiance Bay.
The upper floor contains even more offices, including a large board covered in pictures of persons of interest to the Dunryd Row. Lady Webb's office is in the back, in the master's bedroom, together with a massive desk covered in a thick layer of papers.
Characters
Kurren
is located on the first floor.
Lady Webb
is on the second floor.

random loot

Hall of Reconfiguration
[FS]
Details

Guid
f82790e3-2fd9-4e8f-a900-d885b542715a
Hall of Reconfiguration
is a
location
in
The Forgotten Sanctum
.
Background
The lower level of the Enclosure holds even more creatures and kith too dangerous to be left to roam Eora unchecked. The western wing of the complex is where librarians conduct experiments on the captured creatures.
Points of interest
The table in the Alcove of Incision to the north-west contains a
Fungicide
and a Fungus. You can pick up the Fungus and get 3x
Huona Mahe
. Alternatively, if you decide to convert the Fungicide to
Fungal Fertilizer
with
Alchemy
17 and use that on the Fungus you will get 5x
Huona Mahe
instead. Check the corresponding pages for more uses.
The flesh management device is behind a locked door in the eastern room. If you have the
Maintenance Log - Flesh Construct Controller
, or with 12 Survival or the Ranger class, you can squeeze it hard to kill all the
Augmented Flesh Constructs
in the level or squeeze it softly to make them allies.
Characters
Frightened Child
- in the easternmost cell with the dead bodies lying around. Any attempt to talk with her will result in a fight.
Loot
Scourge of Bezzello
: Dropped by the frightened child, northeastern room.
Ooblit
: In a cage in the Waiting room, just northwestern to the Screaming Pit.
Lucero
: In a cage in the Waiting room, just northwestern to the Screaming Pit.
Fungicide
: Found on the chemistry table in the Alcove of Incision, northwestern room. Can be converted into
Fungal Fertilizer
with
Alchemy
17 (party assisted).
Advanced Wistful Stargazing
: in the Alcove of Incision, bookshelf on northeastern wall.

Hall of the Unseen
Details
Game
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
Type
Ruin
Coordinates
30° 51′ S, 58° 15′ E
Locations
Part of
The Black Isles
Contains
Temple of Revelation
People
Enemies
Spectre
Shade
Quests
Hidden Depths
Miscellaneous
Level
12
Explore XP
60
Critical path
Allows rest?
Technical
Zone ID
zn_26_uncharted_islands
Area ID
AR_2606_Maze_Of_Wael
Guid
a53dce70-3d0c-4c29-a535-9ad7c4a21132
Hall of the Unseen
is a
location
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Background
After an arduous journey, you finally reach the center of the volcanic devastation. Before you is a broad-faced stone building rugged with age.
The eye of Wael, solitary and all-seeing, is emblazoned above the door.
Points of interest
This is a large temple composed of four concentric rings. You enter in the north-easternmost point of the map. Your goal is to reach the central area, but the way in can be tricky. The northeastern door in the second circle can be opened when the three preceding doors are open
and
a character stays behind to activate the eastern plate in the northernmost outer circle.
Then the door to Wael's chamber can be opened by pressing the switch two doors before the entrance, then the northern one in the room before it.
The switch on the wall inside the central chamber opens all doors, but spawns 3 spectres and a shade. Collect
The Eye of Wael
from the central pedestal afterwards, though you can do this beforehand without needing to kill the spectres.
If you have started the quest
Hidden Depths
[FS]
, there is no need to complete the puzzle or fight the spectres. Upon entering the zone, all of the doors will magically open, freeing the way to the central area. Once you reach the center,
Llengrath
will greet you and open the way to the
Temple of Revelation
.
Solution
Note:
This is only one of many possible solutions
The image
in the gallery below
outlines the steps required to open the central room. Simply activate
only
the switches marked, in numerical order. Once your party is at the switch for step 9, leave a character at that switch, then send the rest of the party to stand in the small area containing the two switches for step 10 and 11. Use the character you left behind to activate switch 9, thereby opening the way to the central chamber. To let the remaining party member through, activate switch 10, then 11 (though this isn't required, and it doesn't matter which order you pull these).
Loot
The Eye of Wael
: In the center.
Eliel
: A pet dog, in the inner ring.
Gallery
SI graphic
Puzzle solution

Harbingers' Retreat
[BW]

ed120513-617b-46c9-b81b-73c5420dc447
Harbingers' Retreat
is a
location
in
Beast of Winter
.
Background
The Retreat is the central feature of the Watch. The deceptively small tavern up top houses the entrance to the main temple of the
Harbingers of Dusk
, and the quarters of
Vatnir
, the cult's leader.
Points of interest
The Retreat is fairly large, divided into three major areas. The entrance is where you find Valbrendhur who runs the Tip of the Iceberg, the local tavern and waystation for the few travelers who pass through.
Through the common room lies the temple, carved out of the glacial ice.
Vatnir
is here, presiding over the services.
To the northeast are Vatnir's quarters. The skull has a hidden switch (Perception 12 required), which opens the adjacent secret room, containing some minor loot. Interestingly, it seems nobody has been there in a while.
Characters
High Harbinger
Vatnir
, companion and leader.
Harbinger Valbrendhür
, tavern keeper
Loot
Permafrost shard
: In the rafters in the entrance room (hidden).

Harbingers' Watch
is a
location
in
Beast of Winter
.
Background
This picturesque village was built on the edge of the Dead Floe, a massive glacier that seems to be growing with each passing year. It is inhabited by a peculiar cult of
Rymrgand
, zealously devoted to death and dying, led by an endings godlike called
Vatnir
.
Points of interest
The village is small, centered around
Harbingers' Retreat
, the common house. To the southwest is a statue of Rymrgand create by
Harbinger Leksi
, with
Harbingers' dwelling
below. The fishmonger and brewer offer their wares along the stream to the northeast. The exit deeper into the glacier is located on the northwestern ice wall, and requires
Vatnir's icepicks
to pass through.

Hasongo
Details
Game
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
Type
Town
Coordinates
13° 37′ S, 39° 49′ E
Locations
Part of
Deadfire Archipelago
Contains
Storage tower
Cannon rampart
Dining hall
Kitchen
Engineering workshop
Fleet Master's Office
Lighthouse
Exits to
World map
People
Inhabitants
Zuhira
Sugaan
Aimuro
Bearn
Yanass
Quests
A Distant Light
Miscellaneous
Level
8
Explore XP
40
Critical path
Allows rest?
Technical
Zone ID
zn_11_hasongo
Area ID
AR_1101_Hasongo_Ext
Guid
742661d5-70cb-4e83-bb7b-5d0ea9e9a9ee
Hasongo
is a
location
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Background
Hasongo is a strategic port of the
Royal Deadfire Company
, being a crucial stopover point for food convoys back to
Rauatai
. The recent destruction wrought by Eothas has wiped it out and endangered the lifeline to the heart of the Empire. To make things worse, the heavily damaged and depopulated fort came under attack from Naga, nearly wiping out the Rauatai contingent and the Dawnstars hired by the Fleet Master to handle the fields outside the walls.
Points of interest
You begin the slog through the ruins of the port in the south-eastern corner. Both
Maia Rua
and
Edér
have unique interactions when you arrive. You need to navigate the entire map in order to get through to the lighthouse, so better start early. Move off the pier and fight off the
Coral nāga warrior
(another comes out of the water when you approach the broken boardwalk to the west), then enter the
storage tower
to the northeast. Talk to
Zuhira
to get some intel and learn that there is still an intact bombard on the walls of the fortress.
Head north to the
Cannon rampart
, fighting 3x
Coral nāga archers
on the top of the wall (annoyingly behind a dozen or so barrels), and another warrior by the bombard itself. Movement abilities like
Leap
or
Evasive Roll
work well here to avoid having to deal with the barrels. Just west of the bombard you'll be able to cross over from the cannon rampart roof to the kitchen roof with
Athletics
4 and a
Rope and Grappling Hook
. This helps you avoid being spotted by a nāga warrior on the edge of the water, though you can also just sneak by.
In order to operate the bombard you'll need a few things:
An operator, either with Maia in your party,
Mechanics
10, or with the
Artillerist's Manual
. The manual can be found in the cannon rampart, in the area just under the bombard.
Ammunition
, found in Aimuro's workshop by the northern gate (Zuhira can give you the
key
).
A way to move the swiveling mechanism, to get it pointed in the right direction. Either using someone with at least
Might
18, or with
Cooking Fat
from the kitchen to loosen up the gears.
The naga in the central area cannot have already been fought and defeated. Otherwise the option to fire the cannon never appears.
Once fired, it will make short work of all the nāga in the central area, and will clear the rubble leading to the fleet master's office. If you already fought the naga and cannot fire the cannon you will be unable to damage the rubble which is immune to all known attacks and magic, however you can still access the Fleet Master's office via the northern path, which eventually leads down to the office's second floor and a staircase to the main level.
The northeastern corner contains the
kitchen
and the piers, where you can wipe out some looters for loot. The kitchen has the ladder to the rooftop with a work crane, and the scripted interaction - the submerged corridor:
Going left leads to the hidden part of the rampart where you can find
Yanass
. Require
Might
14 or a
prybar
.
Going right leads to the dining hall, bypassing the group of nāga led by a
Coral nāga shaman
guarding the way across the water. Require an
Athletics
8 check on all party members.
The northwestern sector has the
dining hall
and
engineering workshop
. You can ascend to the ramparts over the northern gate wall, which leads directly to the lighthouse:
Climbing the wall, requiring an
Athletics
8 check on all party members. If any character fails, you will be unable to scale the tower, and the character with the lowest Athletics will get a
Twisted Ankle
(-20 Reflex).
Ascend using a
Rope and Grappling Hook
. However this method will make noise, calling two more nāga warriors jumping out of the water, you can quickly climb the wall before they find you or simply kill them.
The central area crawls with nāga, so you might want to bypass it.
The southwestern part contains the
lighthouse
and the
fleet master's office
. A pathway connects the roof of the engineering workshop to the roof of the office, where a couple of nāga archers await. From the office itself, you can head west up some stairs to reach the lighthouse.
The central-southmost point of the map, by the broken boardwalk and just south of the group of nāga in the center, is empty, but a nāga shaman and warrior will jump out of the water when you approach the totem. You can get a rope and grappling hook from a chest in the corner if you're in need of one.
Characters
Zuhira
, an RDC survivor
Sugaan
, the naga
Aimuro
, an RDC engineer
Bearn
Yanass
, a naga soul
Loot
Corlagon
: At the top of the lighthouse.
Kaz
: Inside the Fleet Master's Office.
Strand of Favor
: Carried by Sugaan.  Note:  you can pickpocket another strand of favor off of Sugaan if you do so before proceeding to restore the Adra pillar.
Exceptional Leather Armor
: On a hidden body, crushed under Eothas' first left foot print.
Bronze Hons
,
Bronze Ōas
,
Old Bronze Ōas
,
Brass Teos
, and
Silver Utokis
,  can be looted from each of the ash-turned corpses dotted around the area.
Gallery
Broken bridge
Bombard
Northern Gate
Crossing the broken section of wall to the north:
The brass bombard to the northeast:
Scaling a wall by the northern gate outside the dining hall:
Climbing up
Climbing down
Climbing with no rope
Failing to climb

🗺️
Hearthsong
Location ID
AR_1101_Hearthstone_Exterior
Loading screen
Hearthsong
is a district of the Glanfathan city,
Twin Elms
, in
Eir Glanfath
.
Background
The commons district of
Twin Elms
and the only one where foreigners are permitted to mingle with Glanfathans (barring extraordinary circumstances).   It's a place where Glanfathans and estramorwn may gather together, as well as the site of the Passage of the Six, where Glanfathan tribal leaders, the anamfatha, meet. Other public areas include the open-air market and the
Celestial Sapling
.
Points of interest
Hearthsong is divided into three distinct levels. The lowest level contains the entrance from
Elmshore
in the southwest and the main gates (you'll be accosted by a Glanfathan guard and informed of the rules that you're expected to follow), with the elevator to the
Celestial Sapling
in the southeast and exit to
Twin Elms
in the northeast. A
Glanfathan home
is also nearby.
In the heart of the area is
the Passage of the Six
, center of governance for Eir Glanfath whenever the anamfatha decide to meet.  The same level has the
Hearthsong Market
in the northwest corner.
The topmost level contains the Builders' Hearth, the passage to
Oldsong
, and two more Glanfathan homes.
Characters
Rinatto
, found near the Celestial Sapling, fuming.
Rotuk
, pulls the lift to
the Celestial Sapling
.
Backer NPCs
Elfwyn Winterwolf
Loralei Rosethorn
Nadam
Pumpkin the Khwarg
Loot
Notable loot
Orange Tabby Cat
: A pet, near the stairs to Builder's Hearth.

For a complete list of fixed and
random loot
found in Hearthsong, see
here
.

(

)

)

Behind the scenes
“
Welcome to Twin Elms. Hearthsong is the commons district of this city, where glanfathans live and mingle and where foreigners are allowed to stay in the city, all other places are forbidden. Being the hangout spot, I designed the place with a couple of things in mind. It had to have an inn, a market place, plenty of dwellings, an an important ritualistic building. Also to emphasise the union of glanfathan and ancient engwithan architecture, the design blends the two architectural styles in a way that seems almost organic.
Sean Dunny - the environment artist that turned the complex 3D blockout into really an amazing 3D render - commented after his first pass on the area that he totally wanted to live in a place like this, and it was awesome to hear that response because that's exactly what I was aiming for when first blocking out the area.
It was also important to give a sense of what glanfathan society was like, and to contrast it immediately with the people of Defiance Bay. This was accomplished with a few key details, for example one, the glanfathans are warlike, pretty much everyone is armed and geared for battle. Two, glanfathans live in relative harmony with other creatures of the wilderness, boasting many rangers and druids, and defend their town with the aid of tame stelgaer prides. Lastly, glanfathans have their own kind of voice and a way of looking at the world which was conveyed in the figures of a speech from the moment that you set foot in the city
”
~
Jorge Salgado, Designer
https://pillarsofeternity.fandom.com/wiki/File:Jorge_salgado_hearthsong_exterior.ogg
Developer commentary by Jorge Salgado
Map
Map - Hearthsong

🗺️
Hearthsong Market
Location ID
AR_1103_Hearthsong_Market
Loading screen
Hearthsong Market
is a market building in the
Hearthsong
district of
Twin Elms
.
Background
The enclosed market of Twin Elms, the heart of its commerce and perhaps the one place where Dyrwoodans and Glanfathans mingle together without any barriers.
Points of interest
This is a single, circular chamber filled with people. There are five merchants across the area, each selling a specific selection of goods.

Heritage Hill
Details
Game
Pillars of Eternity
Type
City District
Locations
Part of
Defiance Bay
Contains
Icantha's House
Mausoleum
Tower
Valtas Manor
Valtas Mausoleum
Exits to
World map
People
Monsters

Quests
Missing Sentries
Rise and Fall
Safe Haven
Undying Heritage
Technical
Map
🗺️
Heritage Hill
Location ID
AR_0501_Heritage_Hill_Exterior
Loading screen
“
What isn't wrong is the real question. A whole district of walking, flesh-eating corpses. We sealed the area two months ago, just after I lost one of my finest commanders there. And yet this is the second guard rotation to disappear.
”
~
Wyla
, when asked what's wrong with the district
Heritage Hill
is a district of
Defiance Bay
in
Pillars of Eternity
.
Background
Once one of the most prestigious districts, Heritage Hill has been under quarantine for two months by the time
the Watcher
arrives in
Defiance Bay
.
Crucible Knight
Wyla
, who trains Novitiates at their
keep
in
First Fires
, has sealed it off as its inhabitants have become mad and started to eat each other. Since then, many visitors to the district and even
guards
have gone
missing
, and rumors circulate about the
undead
and unspeakable horrors.
Indeed, when the Watcher has gained admission and enters Heritage Hill, he encounters all sorts of undead, streets abandoned by their inhabitants, the curbs adorned with corpses, many signs of destruction and an oppressiveness, reminiscent of
Gilded Vale
.
Points of interest
Note:
If you want to have all possible options available for all
quests
here besides
Missing Sentries
, you should not enter the
Tower
,
Icantha's House
or
Valtas Manor
before you've got
a lead
to Heritage Hill.
The entire district is one large war zone. After gaining access (
Might
18,
1,000
, a
key
- looted from the Justiciar nearby, or with
Missing Sentries
active), you will keep fighting undead every step of the way, starting with the abandoned house right behind the gate. To the north lies the cemetery, with a winding alley that leads to a small plaza with a defaced statue  of
Admeth Hadret
. You can pick the  vaults on the way and loot the remnants of the dead for a
Spellward Amulet
and coin. A
mausoleum
lies next to the plaza to the north, with the unique
Valtas Mausoleum
to the south-east.
Along the center of the upper edge of the map lies
Valtas Manor
, with
Icantha's House
in the northeastern corner. Finally, the
Tower
is found in the south-west corner.
Four
backer memorials
are located in the exterior graveyard area, and a further three can be found in the interior mausoleum areas.
Locations
Note:
If you want to have all possible options available for all
quests
here besides
Missing Sentries
, you should not enter the
Tower
,
Icantha's House
or
Valtas Manor
before you've got
a lead
to Heritage Hill.

PAGE: Huana longhouse
CATEGORIES: Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire locations

======================================================================

Huana Longhouse
Details
Game
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
Type
Building
Locations
Part of
Sayuka
People
Inhabitants
Watūri
Osauro
Nedunga
Miscellaneous
Level
10

50
Critical path
Allows rest?
Technical
Zone ID
zn_15_rauatai_port
Area ID
AR_1503_Rauatai_Port_Huana_Longhouse
Guid
fa2d4aae-d2fb-4723-a31c-0083330cf483
Huana longhouse
is a
location
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Background
The longhouse was elected by Rauataians as a form of disaster relief for the Huana living on the island, after a series of storms destroyed their village. Though housing the castes packed together the Rauatai way has stressed a number of Huana, it has also steadily eroded caste divisions leading to new friendships and transfer of skills - though the Mataru once heading the village cannot conceal his disdain for
equality
.
Points of interest
This is a single, long building with characters spread out across. The elitist Mataru occupies the fanciest single quarters at the head of the building.
Characters
Watūri
,
Osauro
, and
Nedunga
Loot
Superb War Hammer
: In a locked chest in
Watūri
's room - requires
Mechanics
13. You can easily grab it while he's out of the room, inspecting the armoire in the hall.
Gallery
Building exterior

PAGE: Hōhina Ravine
CATEGORIES: Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire locations

======================================================================

Hōhina Ravine
Details
Game
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
Type
Wilderness
Coordinates
22° 27′ S, 57° 24′ E
Locations
Part of
Tehiwai Islands

World map

Broodmother's Fury
Miscellaneous
Level
7

35
Critical path
Allows rest?
Technical
Zone ID
zn_01_tikawara
Area ID
AR_0106_Village_Ravine_01_Int
Guid
f954ed18-c44e-4eb1-abf4-b27547c96ad8
Loading screen
Hōhina Ravine
is a
location
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Background
This large, forbidding ravine is currently the spawning ground of a local tribe of lagufaeth, led here after the auamua of
Tikawara
captured a clutch of their young. It's been turned into a large, inaccessible gauntlet of traps and pissed-off lagufaeth.
Points of interest
As expected, this is a  linear slog through two waves of lagufaeth, spiced up by the odd trap or two. There's a small area to the southeast with a delemgan and an assassin vine. You can interact with the altar, and need a fire or freeze spell, or 16 Might to break through the vines and acquire
Vion-ceth
, an Engwithan woodcutting axe.
The waterfall is in the northeastern. Upon approaching it, the lagufaeth broodmother will appear, but strangely won't attack, indicating she does not have hostile intentions towards you. Head west towards the top plateau and you'll be able to talk to her and figure out that she wants you to free the lagufaeth hatchlings at
Tikawara
. You can agree to that in return for the tribe calling off its campaign of aggression against the village.
Enemies
Wilder
Lagufaeth
Lagufaeth mage
(6x)
Lagufaeth sidewinder
(x4)
Lagufaeth redfin
(x2)
Lagufaeth broodmother
Beasts
Tamed scarab beetle
Primordial
Assassin vine
Delemgan
Jungle lurker
Notable loot
Vion-ceth
: In the overgrown stone chest guarded by a delemgan.
Baubles of the Fin
: Can be looted from the broodmother, or received as a quest reward,
Gallery
Altar scripted interaction

PAGE: Ibrel's Well
CATEGORIES: Pillars of Eternity mentioned-only locations
URL: https://pillarsofeternity.fandom.com/wiki/Ibrel's_Well
======================================================================

Ibrel's Well
is a ruined ancient
Glanfathan
city in eastern
Eir Glanfath
. It lies in the forest due east of
Solace Vale
, northeast of
Midwood
, and west of
Maiden Falls
. It bears religious significance to the locals, the closest of which is the tribe of
Twice-Split Arrows
. Travellers in the area tend to avoid trails leading near the well due to the presence of
pŵgra
and other creatures, who often stalk the paths for prey.
Trivia
The reknowned explorer
Erol of Levi
visited the Well on his travels in 2665
AI
. A painting of the ruins can be seen hanging at
The Goose and Fox
in
Copperlane
.
References

PAGE: Icantha's House

Icantha's House
Details
Game
Pillars of Eternity
Type
House
Locations
Part of
Heritage Hill

People

Technical
Map
🗺️
Icantha's House
Location ID
AR_0509_Heritage_Hill_Ground_01
Icantha's House
is a building in
Heritage Hill
.
Background
This torn down house belongs to
Icantha
, a scientist who managed to survive death itself by dying. It's falling apart slowly and is host to her own brand of horror.
Points of interest
Icantha is in the main room, by the fireplace and offers some speculation as to what's happening in the district.
The rooms beyond can be explored at leisure. The first contains Icantha's bedroom with lootable containers. The other room can be picked open and contains her 'cattle', humans used to nourish her vessel and stave off descent into madness.
You can free the prisoners in the back room for a Minor Positive reputation gain with Defiance Bay.
Alternatively during
Undying Heritage
, if you spoke to
Aldhelm
at the top of the tower and learned of his hunger, you can trick the prisoners and send them to sate his hunger. This allows you to absorb the power of the souls and gain the talent
Gift from the Machine
.
Characters
Icantha
a surviving but ghoulified scientist.
Loot
Note:
For a complete list of
random loot
found in Icantha's House, see
here
.

PAGE: Imperial Command

Imperial Command
Details
Game
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
Type

Sabormi

Clearing Out Crookspur

The Brass Empire
The Final Maneuver
Miscellaneous
Level
6
Explore XP
30
Critical path
Allows rest?
Technical
Zone ID
zn_08_rauatai_district
Area ID
AR_0805_Rauatai_HQ
AR_0806_Rauatai_HQ_Basement
Guid
eee8ae78-2d2a-44bb-bc3a-1b41478fe8b4
1dda2a0a-49fc-484b-857e-22412b3c4ca8
Imperial Command
is a
location
in
the Brass Citadel
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Background
The nerve center of Rauataian operations in the Deadfire, Imperial Command holds the main quartermastery, hazanui's office, meeting room, and training facilities for its most elite troops. It is built in the characteristic style of modern Rauataian architecture, but the beauty does not inhibit function.
Points of interest
Upper level
The upper floor is where the office of the hazanui is located, opposite the entrance. The quartermastery and
Sabormi
is located in the right wing, next to the stairs leading downward.
Lower level
The lower level is where the elite troops of the RDC are quartered. The rooms are arranged clockwise around the central stairwell and include Grand Secretary
Atsura
's office, the war room, jail, exit to dock, kitchen, and barracks. The barracks are particularly notable for a high amount of stealable loot.
The exit on the southern side of this floor leads to an external walkway and shortcut to the main floor. At the top of the stairs is the orphaned dog
Pes
.
Characters
Hazanui Karū
Sabormi
, the quartermaster
Grand Secretary
Atsura
Verzano
, overseer of "Sanitary Engineering".
Only appears if he is not killed during
At All Costs
in
Pillars of Eternity
.
Gallery
Building exterior

CATEGORIES: Locations, Pillars of Eternity mentioned-only locations
URL: https://pillarsofeternity.fandom.com/wiki/Ine_Sycthrúa
======================================================================

Ine Sycthrúa
(
IH-nah SEEK-throo-ah
,
Aedyran
:
Pearl Sepulchers
), is a unique
adra
formation in the
Aedyr
, discovered in approximately 2000
AI
. It consists of a circle of large adra standing stones.
When fercönyngs and mecwyns (the kings and queens of the
Aedyr Empire
) are approaching death, they do everything they can to die in the circle. Those who die in the circle do not pass on to the other side, but are bound into the stones and can collectively, often in cryptic or confused ways, communicate to living beings in their midst (typically only the fercönyng or mecwyn).
This allows for the emergence of a "council of the dead", as imperial couples are laid to the rest at the site, creating a cryptic advisory body for the future leaders of the Empire.
References
