======================================================================
PAGE: Weald of Fates
CATEGORIES: Pillars of Eternity locations, Pillars of Eternity wildernesses
URL: https://pillarsofeternity.fandom.com/wiki/Weald_of_Fates
======================================================================

Cut content
This article or section refers to content that has been cut from the final release of the game.
It may include partially developed content, content existing within game files but not fully present or usable in game, and/or early and unreleased concepts.
Weald of Fates
Details
Game
Pillars of Eternity
Type
Wilderness
Locations
Part of
Twin Elms
Exits to
Elms' Reach
Technical
Location ID
AR_1302_Weald_Of_Fates
Weald of Fates
is a cut wilderness area in
Twin Elms
, the glanfathan city in
Pillars of Eternity
. It was to be accessed via an archway on the eastern side of
Elms' Reach
.
Background
The Weald is a mystical and magical forest containing ancestor stones, a collection of souls of glanfathan leaders, whose wisdom is revered by the tribes. In order to find and speak with the stones, a tribesman would ingest hallucinogenic mushrooms which would guide them on a "vision quest".
Behind the scenes
The Weald is mentioned in the developer commentary upon entering the Elms' Reach district. It was cut during development in order to focus on polishing existing areas of the game.
“
...At its bottom right corner, you'll notice perhaps a cool archway made by roots from one of the elms. This led to an area that we had to cut, a mystical forest called the Weald of Fates where you'd be sent to speak with the ancestor stones, a collection of souls of glanfathan leaders, whose wisdom is revered by the tribes. The catch was of course that you could only do so by ingesting hallucinogenic mushrooms that would lead you "vision quest" towards its stones. The realities of game development sometimes result in awesome things like this having to get cut so that you can focus polish into something else, some other areas of the game.
”
~
Jorge Solgado, Designer
Iswld
in the Golden Grove area of Elms Reach has dialogue entries that involve the preparation of
snowcap
used to access the area, as well as for when you return from the area.
"I do, although it is for the safety of overconfident aspirants as much as for the peace of the ancestors. The Weald has a way of denying those deemed unworthy.
"However, Bethwl has sent word that you require a special audience."
"I congratulate you, estramor - you have completed a journey that few are capable of."
"Bethwl will be eager to hear of what you've learned. If her suspicions are correct, the words of the ancestors hold great portent not only for you, but also for the future of Eir Glanfath."
"I have prepared the snowcap according to ritual. But be warned." She holds up a finger. "Though some portion of the journey will take place in your mind, the dangers you face in the Weald can still claim your life.
"The journey will begin whenever you choose, but it will not end until you have spoken with the ancestors or perished in the attempt."
"May your journey be instructive." She removes an oiled leather pouch from her robes. As she opens it, a heavy, musky odor assails you. The grove begins to spin, finally fading into darkness.
When at last you come to your senses, you stand in an unfamiliar forest. The ground beneath your feet feels solid, and you do not think you are imagining the wet chill in the air.
Some references to the area still exist, but are unused. This includes the following quest items:
Filled Water Jug
Glittering Green Gem
Mossy Rock
Stick
Sword of the Weald
Water Jug
The idea of using hallucinogens to guide the party through an area may have been carried forward into
Zahua
's companion quest
Secrets of the Tacan
[WM2]
, where the Watcher can experience visions in
Whitestone Hollow
with the help of Zahua's drug mixture.
References

Whakupoko Lowveld
[SSS]
Details
Game
Seeker, Slayer, Survivor
Type
Wilderness
Coordinates
13° 52′ S, 32° 03′ E
Locations
Part of
Kazuwari
Exits to
World map
Miscellaneous
Level
-1
Explore XP
-
Critical path
Allows rest?
Technical
Zone ID
lax01_worldmapencounters
Area ID
RE_LAX01_Arena_Slayer_Plains
Guid
2b18c0bd-6d0a-45ef-bf8b-a29e58028615
Whakupoko Lowveld
is a
location
in
Seeker, Slayer, Survivor
.
Background
The forest thins into scattered glades along the western cliffs of Kazuwari. Trees stand sentinel overlooking the sheer stone plunge into the Deadfire Sea. To the north lies a large open field ringed with woods and peppered with small clumps of dense forest. Clouds of insects hum over the rotting remnants of discarded carcasses.
Points of interest
Before you enter the lowveld, you are treated to a scripted interaction, allowing you to explore the situation. With Survival 10, you can tell apart the tracks surrounding the carcasses, allowing you to track both beast and kith. Survival 13 and 12 respectively allows you to figure out the tracks were left by a large stelgaer and four kith it was stalking. With Survival 13, you can track the kith tracks over to a scene of rather unpleasant carnage. Examining it yields a moderate amount of loot. Then you can choose to follow either the wounded kith's or the unwounded kith's tracks - or try and commune with the souls.
Tracking the unwounded kith reveals he has fallen of a cliff & died. You can search his corpse for valuables then you have the option to follow the wounded one.
Tracking the wounded kith leads to a dying dwarf clutching a small sack in his hand. After examining his wounds, you can either put him out of his misery, heal him (benevolent), hurt him (cruel), or set up a trap. He's too far gone to be healed, so it's a matter of time before the predator returns. You can set up a trap by using magical abilities, use Mechanics for traps (13 allows for setting up a burning ground one and hide it).
If you return to find the beast, you will hear rustiling with Perception 14 and will require Insight 13 to figure out what it means. Fleeing the beasts is possible with Athletics 9 (any character who doesn't meet that requirement will be at risk of injury). Finally, you will need to either confront the enemies or use Sleight of Hand 15 to give them the slip. Failing causes another wound - and you will fight the Great Stelgaer, accompanied by a several other kitties.
The defeated stelgaer drops the
cindershard
. Immediately after collecting it,
Whehami
appears.
Characters
Whehami
: A facet of the slayer.
Loot
Cindershard
: Dropped by the great stelgaer.

Loading screen
Whitestone Hollow
is a location in
The White March - Part II
.
During
Zahua
's companion quest,
Secrets of the Tacan
, half of the area thaws out. During this time, the
Ice Cave
in the northeast and
Haddi's Cabin
in the southeast becomes inaccessible, and the party cannot exit.
Background
This large stretch of wilderness is dominated by an unique geological formation, the Whitestone Lake, with five ice pillars rising up from the depths.
Points of interest
You enter the area from the northwest. Just to the southeast is the submerged lake containing five ice pillars rising from the surface of the waters. In the northeastern corner is an entrance to a
cavern
that contains the remains of adventurers and locals foolish enough to come across the Hollow's many monsters.
In the south-eastern corner lies a small dock that used to belong to
Haddi
.
Kosta
and
Brys
stand nearby. The
house
nearby is tied to the
Bleak Oath
quest.
The southern shore of the lake is dominated by a large group of
lagufaeths
and
ice blights
. Caution is recommended.
In the south-western corner is the
Terror of Whitestone Hollow
, target of a
bounty
, near the avalanche that wiped out a caravan recently. A group of looters led by
Täog
also lurks nearby. Täog drops the unique
Executioner's Hood
.
A group of
skuldrs
are outside the cave in the northeast. Inside, you can find
Mylla
for the
Wild Mylla
quest.
Finally, in the northwest, is a plateau accessible up several flights of stairs that contains
bears
and the
soulbound
Ryona's Breastplate
.
Characters
Kosta
and
Brys
, onlookers next to Haddi's Cabin.

Bounty: The Terror of Whitestone Hollow
.

.

,

Zahua
's past which manifest as a result of the hallucinogens he (and the party) injests during the quest.

Woodend Plains
Location ID
AR_0811_Woodend_Plains
Loading screen
Woodend Plains
is a wilderness area in
Pillars of Eternity
. It can be accessed as soon as you finish repairing the
Eastern Barbican
in
Caed Nua
.
Background
A stretch of open land on the border of the northern forests, right outside
Defiance Bay
.
Points of interest
The area is defined by the highway crossing leading to
Defiance Bay
and the lands to the south. Your first order of business is to go south, following the trail, and talking to
Sagani
to round out your party.
To the east lies an exit to
Stormwall Gorge
. Directly north is a small mesa with a dead adventurer.
Right off the path to the northwest is a sizable band of
lurkers
led by a
forest spirit
. Best steer clear at early levels unless you enjoy a challenge. Further northwest is an elevated mesa with several
lions
and a shrine to Lover's Tides. The dead peasant carries the unique
hatchet
Hearth's Harvest
. Once you find
Ryona's Breastplate
, a group of
Bleak Walkers
spawns near the mesa.
To the southwest, near the exit to
Defiance Bay
, lies a small, abandoned homestead.
Iben
,
Eagar
,
Beacwof
, and
Venka
are encountered here if you agree to recover the
Scroll of Wael
.
You might want to avoid exiting the map or taking a look to the south – later in
Defiance Bay
there's an
opportunity
to reveal that area per dialog or thievery, which isn't available if discovered now.
Foemyna
can be found below the eastern cliffs if her
bounty
is active.
Characters
Sagani
: potential companion, found at the crossroads.
Iben
,
Eagar
,
Beacwof
, and
Venka
: Appear during
The Parable of Wael
by the farmhouse in the southwest. You have to defeat the thieves to recover the
scroll of Wael
.
Nedyn
: She will be found here if you turn her in to
Grimda
at the end of
The Theorems of Pandgram
.
The
Giantslayers
, led by
Byne "The Nimble"
, appear on the southeastern plateau near the wagon, and confront you during
The Bronze Beneath the Lake
.
Medreth
: A bounty hunter, if you led him astray during
Cat and Mouse
.
Foemyna
: Found in the eastern part of the map during the task
Bounty: Foemyna
.
Backer NPCs
Jaelyn Di'Mathe
Enemies
Beasts:
Lions
Lion

Elder Lion
Primordials:
Delemgans
Pŵgra

random loot
found in Woodend Plains, see

.

(

)

Behind the scenes
This area contains developer commentary.
“
The thing about Woodend Plains is that it needed to be plains, but it couldn't just be some big open space, because then you could just get lost. It would also look bad, so we put in a lot of small obstacles and terrain changes to break up the space. The rock walls and fencing areas were put in to give some directionality to where the player could and couldn't go, and cliffs and plateaus were added to show elevation changes since rolling hills don't really show up very well in isometric perspective.
”
~
Denise McMurry, Level Designer
https://pillarsofeternity.fandom.com/wiki/File:Dini_mcmurry_woodend_plains.ogg
Developer commentary by Denise McMurry
Map
Map - Woodend Plains

Workshop
is a
location
in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
.
Background
The workshop is the heart of the research effort at
Sayuka
, where the brightest minds of Rauatai work to unlock the secrets of the Archipelago - and more. The coralmasons try to uncover the secret of the coral growing so large in the vicinity of the island, while the engineers are... Well, the engineers are engineering a submarine.
Points of interest
A single, large room with coralmasons and engineers. Once you've delivered
Iverra
, she will immediately correct the coralmason who tries to kick you are that you are, in fact, an invited guest. If
Overgrowth
is active, she will also provide you with
Diving Equipment
to reach
Cignath Mór
.
Characters

, an employee of the RDC and assistant to Iverra.

A Matter of Import

, scientist

======================================================================
PAGE: Wāpau Jungle
CATEGORIES: Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire locations
URL: https://pillarsofeternity.fandom.com/wiki/Wāpau_Jungle
======================================================================

Wāpau Jungle
Details
Game
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
Type
Scripted interaction
Coordinates
20° 30′ S, 65° 13′ E
Locations
Part of
Deadfire Archipelago
Contains
Swamp
Exits to
World map
Technical
Area ID
2604_campsite_skaen
Wāpau Jungle
is a scripted interaction in
Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire
. It is located at the eastern edge of the map, near
Subterranean temple
, which itself is north-east of
Poko Kohara Ruins
. Specifically, it can be found at the coordinates
20° 30′ S, 65° 13′ E
.
Background
The fetid, sweltering jungle looms around and above you, the thick canopy capturing the heat. Sweat clings to your nostrils and drips from your chin. Nothing seems to move in all of the world save you.
Your companions' complaints fell silent hours ago.
Save for Serafen, who summons up a new creative objection every ten minutes or so.
Interaction
You and your party venture into the hot, humid jungle on the island. After hours of tireless walking, you stop at a stagnant waterway to refill your water skins when you hear movement in the wilds ahead of you.
A trio of kith emerge from the trees and greet you as the Watcher of Caed Nua. The central figure, a savannah folk explains that they have awaited your arrival, and have a proposition for you, given you step to the side - away from your party - to hear it.
You can move closer to listen (Shady). Moving closer to listen, the man lowers his voice:
"There exists a place, hidden among these wilds... an old place, watched by
the Quiet Slave
. For one willing to make a sacrifice..."
"A true sacrifice," says the woman at his side as she nods towards your companions.
"...such devotion shall be rewarded by him in turn."
Offer a "counter proposal", and attack (Aggressive).
Say you'll be fine right where you are. After a confirmation, this dismisses them and ends the interaction with no outcome.
Alternatively, if the Watcher is a
Priest
of Skaen, or if they distributed the souls to
Woedica
(in the quest
Memories of the Ancients
during the events of
Pillars of Eternity
) the kith will instead come to you with open arms and present you with the effigy as a "gift for your loyalty. And for your resentment". For any other, Skaen would have "made demands" (a sacrifice of the blood of a companion). Accepting the effigy doll ends the interaction.
At any time during the interaction with the kith you can choose to attack them instead of following along. This will place you in the
Jungle
staging area, versus the Skaenite cultists - an extremely easy fight at most levels. Once they're dead you can loot the
Pathfinder's Boots
from the Skaenite Priest.
If you accept the trio's proposal you'll have to choose one of your companions to accompany you.
With your companion in tow, a Survival 12 check will determine if you can follow their vague directions. Failing or succeeding will still get you to the destination, but the former will pass 5 hours and will give you and your companion
Fatigue
(-5
Accuracy
, -10 All
Defenses
, -15% Max Health).
The destination is a temple hidden in the depths of the wilds, heavy stones set against one another and the blank rock face of the mountain. The center of the temple is a stone altar grooved with ancient serrations and stained with long-dried blood.
Sacrifice your companion (Cruel). You kill your companion, and in a rather gruesome manner reach into their chest and  "pull from the body a doll, a figure of wooden sticks bound together, the eyes a pair of blackstones." This gives you the
Effigy Doll
. Returning to your party, they question what became of your chosen companion. Bluffing "they didn't make it" will result in -1 Reputation with
Aloth
(Shady), otherwise the other dialogue options have no adverse affects.
Destroy the altar (Aggressive, Benevolent). You and your companion destroy the shrine, leaving it as a pile of broken rocks and rubble. This ends the interaction with no rewards.
Trivia
If you have no companions in your party, this location is unavailable and will disappear from the map.
When you have to choose which companion will accompany you, you can instead choose yourself - this will evoke a response from one of the kith, who admires your choice but asks you to choose again.
You cannot choose a Hired Adventurer to accompany you, the trio will simply refuse and ask you to try again.
This interaction bares resemblance to the
Dyrford Ruins
blood pool from
Pillars of Eternity
, in which you are able to sacrifice one of your party. However, unlike this, you do not get a unique permanent bonus.
Gallery
Scripted interaction graphics

Yenwood Field
is a wilderness area in
Pillars of Eternity
. It is accessible during
The Battle of Yenwood Field
, appearing on the world map east of
Caed Nua
.
Background
This large field in central
Dyrwood
is the site of
The Battle of Yenwood Field
as
the Watcher
decisively seizes the right to
Caed Nua
from the challenger.
Points of interest
This is a large, open map, depicting the terminal stage of the battle, with
Lord Gathbin
and his elite guard on the right side and your forces on the left side.
Characters
Lord
Arledr Gathbin
Emery
(if you didn't kill her during
A Return to Court
)
Notable loot
Gathbin Family Signet
,
Gauntlets of Ogre Might
,
Blunting Belt
,
Ring of Unshackling
: Dropped by the good lord.
Gallery
The battle underway
